title: Three Lectures On Topological Phases Of Matter

section_map.title: Relativistic Dispersion In One Space Dimension
section_map.summary: In 1+1 dimensions, gamma_0 gamma_1 is the chirality operator and its eigenvalue distinguishes right-moving from left-moving gapless modes; source anchor: Lecture One / Relativistic Dispersion In One Space Dimension (lines 328-350; eqs. Dirac, gamma, hamdir).

section_map.title: Three Dimensions
section_map.summary: For a generic 2x2 Hermitian Hamiltonian, equal eigenvalues impose three real conditions, so isolated crossings are natural in three momentum dimensions; source anchor: Lecture One / Three Dimensions (lines 360-394; eqs. generic, specil, ecial).

section_map.title: The Berry Connection
section_map.summary: The local node charge equals the Berry-flux integral over a small sphere, and the Bianchi identity then re-derives zero net charge; source anchor: Lecture One / The Berry Connection (lines 603-609; eqs. zif, nna).

section_map.title: Edge States And Anomaly Inflow
section_map.summary: On a manifold with boundary, the variation of the bulk Chern-Simons term cannot be canceled by a local boundary counterterm and therefore requires anomalous gapless boundary modes; source anchor: Lecture Two / Edge States And Anomaly Inflow (lines 1600-1629; eqs. facs, trytry).

section_map.title: Joining Valence And Conduction Bands
section_map.summary: Under alpha -> alpha + 2pi, spectral flow along edge branches transfers one unit of charge per right-mover and minus one per left-mover, so k = n_+ - n_-; source anchor: Lecture Two / The Charge Pump and Joining Valence And Conduction Bands (lines 1635-1753; eqs. zimmo, trof).

section_map.title: More On The Fractional Quantum Hall Effect
section_map.summary: Because the classical equation sets f = 0, the relevant gauge-invariant observables are Wilson loops W_s(l) = exp(i s integral_l a) rather than local fields; source anchor: Lecture Three / More On The Fractional Quantum Hall Effect (lines 1976-1989; eqs. doof, ooff).

section_map.title: More On Edge States Of The Integer Quantum Hall Effect
section_map.summary: Integrating out a massive 2+1-dimensional Dirac fermion yields (sign m / 2) I_CS(A), with the half-integer coefficient explained by the hemisphere Berry-winding argument; source anchor: Lecture Three / More On Edge States Of The Integer Quantum Hall Effect (lines 2064-2091; eqs. utof, uftof, puft, PPP).

section_map.title: Haldane's Model Of Graphene
section_map.summary: The honeycomb nearest-neighbor hopping model gives H = t [[0, 1 + e^{-i alpha} + e^{-i beta}], [1 + e^{i alpha} + e^{i beta}, 0]] in the A/B basis; source anchor: Lecture Three / Haldane's Model Of Graphene (lines 2192-2203; eq. hham).

section_map.title: A System With Many Bands
section_map.summary: For an N-band system with k filled bands, the occupied subspace H'_p defines a map into Gr(k,N) away from crossings of the k-th and k+1-th bands; source anchor: Lecture One / A System With Many Bands (lines 742-766).

section_map.title: Weyl Fermions And Fermi Arcs
section_map.summary: A half-space Weyl fermion admits boundary data M psi = psi with M = sigma_2 cos alpha + sigma_3 sin alpha, chosen so that H remains Hermitian; source anchor: Lecture One / Weyl Fermions And Fermi Arcs (lines 883-899; eqs. goodbc, mis, zeff).

section_map.title: Gapless Boundary Modes From Dirac Fermions
section_map.summary: For a Dirac fermion in a half-space, psi = exp(m x_1) psi_parallel is normalizable only for m < 0 and yields a localized 2+1-dimensional boundary Dirac mode; source anchor: Lecture One / Gapless Boundary Modes From Dirac Fermions (lines 1020-1056; eqs. molof, ogg, pogg, zuffo).

section_map.title: Quantization Of The Chern-Simons Coupling
section_map.summary: Because the path-integral phase only requires the action mod 2pi, the coefficient of I_CS must be integral in a microscopic theory with properly quantized charge; source anchor: Lecture Two / Quantization Of The Chern-Simons Coupling (lines 1352-1367).

section_map.title: Relation To Band Topology
section_map.summary: For a gapped two-dimensional band insulator, the integer k' is the first Chern class of the filled-band bundle, equivalently the integral of Tr F / 2pi over the Brillouin zone; source anchor: Lecture Two / Relation To Band Topology (lines 1429-1451).

section_map.title: Proof Of The Equivalence
section_map.summary: Integrating over the little twist-angle torus while summing shifted momenta reorganizes the same calculation as integrating over the full Brillouin zone, so hat k' = k'; source anchor: Lecture Two / Proof Of The Equivalence (lines 1523-1537).

section_map.title: More On Fermi Arcs
section_map.summary: The product U x U' around a projected Weyl point defines a gapped auxiliary 2D system whose Chern number k* forces edge states and hence Fermi arcs in the original 3D problem; source anchor: Lecture Two / More On Fermi Arcs (lines 1788-1846).
